node.text: Today's #GoogleDoodle encourages you to look up at the stars. Head to our homepage to see photos from @NASA's James Webb Space Telescope, including the deepest infrared photo of the universe ever taken 🤯 These awe-inspiring images will help astronomers study every phase of cosmic history, which spans a massive 13.5 billion years, and the mysteries beyond our galaxy.

node.text:
“Pictures tell us about the world, so it’s important that they do that with a sense of accuracy and beauty that maps back to our real experiences.” — Florian Koenigsberger 

Florian leads our Image Equity Initiative, helping to make sure Pixel cameras capture everyone as they truly are. Learn more about our efforts to build a world where everyone belongs at the link in bio.

node.text: This #TeacherAppreciationWeek and every week we're thankful for all the educators helping future generations grow. You can thank an educator for their encouragement, inspiration and support by sending one of the ready-to-share thank you cards from today’s Instagram Story. Tap the link in bio to hear @CCSSO National Teacher Of The Year’s Kurt Russell share his story, the importance of the teaching community and ways we're showing.

node.text: "After it became clear that Live Transcribe helps me to communicate, it became clear that we could extend it to help others,” Googler Dimitri Kanevsky says about his work as a speech research scientist. He’s developing technology like Live Transcribe to help people fully participate in the conversations around them. Learn more about our efforts to build a world where everyone belongs at the link in bio.

node.text: Wonder how the judges score @teamusa’s @amberglenniceskater? ⛸️ Swipe for the why behind the numbers via Search.

node.accessibility_caption: An image of figure skater Amber Glenn wearing a black costume. Overlaid on the image is a search bar containing the text, “explain the scoring for figure skating like i’m 5.” The Google “G,” an American flag, and the Olympic rings appear at the bottom left with the text, “Official Partner of Team USA.”

A slide with a text bubble at the top reading, “explain the scoring for figure skating like i’m 5.” The main body text reads: “Imagine figure skating scoring is like getting a grade on a big school project that has two different parts: what you did and how pretty it looked.” A small image of Amber Glenn performing a jump appears at the bottom.

A slide titled “1. The Technical Score (The ‘What’ You Did).” The introductory text reads: “This part is like a checklist. Every time a skater does a trick – like a jump or a spin – they get ‘starting points’ just for trying it.” The slide includes two bullet points:

Base Value: Harder tricks are worth more “starting points.” A jump with four spins in the air (a quad) is worth way more than a jump with only one spin.
The “Plus or Minus” (Grade of Execution): After the skater does the trick, the judges decide how good it was. If it was perfect, they add extra points (up to +5). If the skater wobbled or fell, they take points away (up to -5).

A slide titled “2. The Artistic Score (The ‘How’ You Did It).” The introductory text reads: “This is for the parts between the big tricks. Judges look at three main things to give a score from 0 to 10.” The slide features an image of Amber Glenn in a crouched pose and three bullet points:

Skating Skills: How fast and smooth they glide on the ice.
Presentation: How well they show their feelings and “dance” to the music.
Composition: How the whole routine was put together, like a well-drawn picture.

A slide titled “3. Oops! Deductions.” The introductory text reads: “Just like losing a sticker for breaking a rule, skaters lose points for big mistakes.” It features an image of Amber Glenn reaching for the ice and two bullet points:

Falls: Every time a skater falls down, they lose 1 full point.
Music/Time/Style: If the music is too long or they wear something they aren’t supposed to, they lose points too.

A slide titled “How do they win?” featuring an image of Amber Glenn skating on one skate. The body text reads: “Skaters usually perform two different routines: a short one and a long one. The judges add up all the points from both. Whoever has the biggest pile of points at the end of the day wins the trophy!”

node.accessibility_caption: A handmade Valentine’s card featuring three layered paper hearts in red, pink, and white. The innermost white heart has the handwritten message, "Thanks for being you!" in red ink.
A pink paper heart decorated with a thick border of purple and pink sequins. Inside, the message "YOU’RE SEW COOL!" is handwritten in red, surrounded by small brown "stitch" marks.
A white paper heart featuring a hand-drawn illustration of two dachshunds (one brown, one tan) in a green field under a pink heart-filled sky. It reads, "MY HEART LOOOONGS FOR YOU!"
A simple red paper heart with a darker red border. The words "you're THE GOAT" are written in black marker, with "GOAT" underlined for emphasis.
A purple paper heart with a hand-drawn smiley face wearing pink glasses. The message "So cool you Rule!" is written in blue ink.
A red paper heart with yellow and orange paper flames erupting from the top and bottom edges. The center features the handwritten text, "You light up my heart!"
A pink paper heart with a dark purple border. It is decorated with small clusters of  red hearts. The center, written in marker, reads, "I’m so lucky to call you my friend."
A pinkish red paper heart with red glitter yarn "stitched" around the edge and tied into a bow at the top. The message "You make my heart smile" is handwritten in blue ink.
A light pink paper heart with multiple pink marker heart outlines drawn inside. In the center, "you're my BFF" is written in bright pink marker.
A light pink paper heart decorated with red fuzzy pipe cleaner heart shapes. The words "BE MINE" are written in the center using pink and red glitter glue.
A peach-colored paper heart covered in small, stamped purple and pink hearts. The central message reads, "Even miles apart you’re close to my heart."
A bright pink paper heart with a border of iridescent white pearls and pink sequins. The message "You have my heart" is written in elegant red cursive script.
